messages.content: 翻译为中文：(1) A single-controller architecture combined with an appropriate abstraction of the Parallel Worker simplifies the development of the train- ing pipeline. (2) The Parallel Strategy and Data Transfer modules enable efficient and scalable training. (3) The Rollout Scheduler offers fine-grained management of each sample’s lifecycle during the rollout generation stage. (4) The Environment Worker and Reward Worker support rapid and flexible experimentation with agentic RL algo- rithms and reward designs, respectively. (5) AutoDeviceMapping allows users to assign resources to different models across various stages flexibly

(1) 单控制器架构结合对并行 Worker（工作进程）的适当抽象，简化了训练流水线的开发。  
(2) 并行策略（Parallel Strategy）与数据传输（Data Transfer）模块支持高效且可扩展的训练。  
(3) Rollout 调度器（Rollout Scheduler）在 rollout 生成阶段对每个样本的生命周期提供细粒度管理。  
(4) 环境 Worker（Environment Worker）与奖励 Worker（Reward Worker）分别支持对具智能体特性的强化学习算法（agentic RL algorithms）以及奖励设计的快速、灵活实验。  
(5) 自动设备映射（AutoDeviceMapping）使用户能够在不同阶段将资源灵活分配给不同模型，并可跨多种设备进行部署。

Fast and Cost Effective: ROLL can fully exploit the high-performance hardware resources to expedite  
the RL training and achieve considerable training cost and time reduction in a large GPU cluster.  
• Scalability and Fault Tolerance: ROLL supports a wide range of LLM training and serving optimization  
techniques, enabling the scalable training of a 200B-parameter model across thousands of GPUs  
without interruption for about two weeks. It also features an efficient checkpoint and resumption  
mechanism, allowing the training task to be restarted with minimal engineering effort.  
• Flexible Hardware Usage: ROLL supports running RL training across a variety of hardware types.  
Users can choose between colocation or disaggregation, and configure synchronous or asynchronous  
execution modes, to fully leverage the advantages of different hardware architectures.

• **快速且具成本效益：** ROLL 能够充分挖掘高性能硬件资源以加速强化学习（RL）训练，从而在大型 GPU 集群中大幅降低训练成本并缩短训练时间。

• **可扩展性与容错性：** ROLL 支持广泛的大语言模型（LLM）训练与推理（serving）优化技术，能够实现在数千张 GPU 上对 2000 亿（200B）参数模型进行为期约两周的持续无中断扩展训练。此外，它还具备高效的检查点（checkpoint）与断点续训机制，允许以极低的工程成本重启训练任务。

• **灵活的硬件配置：** ROLL 支持在多种类型的硬件上进行强化学习训练。用户可以选择共置（colocation）或分离（disaggregation）部署方案，并配置同步或异步执行模式，以充分发挥不同硬件架构的优势。

Diverse and Extensible Rewards/Environments: ROLL implements a set of Reward Workers and  
Environment Workers. Product developers can readily customize their own reward and environment  
by building upon our in-situ implementations.  
• Compositional Sample-Reward Route: ROLL provides a user-friendly interface to control the prompt  
sampling ratio across different tasks and dynamically route each sample to the corresponding Reward  
Worker (e.g., mathematical verifiers, sandbox environments, and LLM-as-a-judge). As production-  
level LLMs often encompass a diverse range of capabilities, this feature enables developers to optimize  
model performance across a mixture of domains and tasks.  
• Easy Device-Reward Mapping: ROLL develops the device-reward mapping interface to easily config-  
ure the device mapping of the Reward Worker. This feature isolates the reward computation from  
other computational workloads in multi-task RL training for LLMs, preventing interference and  
performance bottlenecks.  
• Rich Training Recipes: ROLL provides various RL algorithms, LLMs, tasks, and datasets to reduce the  
engineering effort required for developing new training features.  
• Superior Performance: ROLL consists of a set of tuned training configurations that reach satisfactory  
performance across many tasks, relieving the burden of laborious hyperparameter search

• **多样化且可扩展的奖励/环境：** ROLL 实现了一组奖励 Worker（Reward Workers）和环境 Worker（Environment Workers）。产品开发者可以基于我们的内置实现，便捷地定制他们自己的奖励和环境。

• **组合式样本-奖励路由：** ROLL 提供了一个用户友好的接口，用于控制不同任务间的提示词（Prompt）采样比例，并将每个样本动态路由至相应的奖励 Worker（例如数学验证器、沙箱环境以及作为评判者的大语言模型 LLM-as-a-judge）。由于生产级 LLM 通常涵盖广泛的能力，该特性使开发者能够针对混合领域和任务优化模型性能。

• **便捷的设备-奖励映射：** ROLL 开发了设备-奖励映射接口，以便轻松配置奖励 Worker 的设备映射。该特性在 LLM 的多任务 RL 训练中将奖励计算与其他计算负载隔离开来，从而防止干扰和性能瓶颈。

• **丰富的训练配方：** ROLL 提供了各种 RL 算法、LLM、任务和数据集，以减少开发新训练特性所需的工程工作量。

• **卓越的性能：** ROLL 包含一套经过调优的训练配置，可在许多任务中达到令人满意的性能，从而减轻了繁重的超参数搜索负担。

Constrained Device Execution: ROLL enables efficient training on constrained GPU resources via  
a set of memory optimization techniques, including single-GPU setups. This allows algorithm  
researchers to conduct multiple trial-and-error experiments and obtain timely feedback without  
requiring extensive high-grade GPUs.  
• Pluggable Reasoning Pipeline: ROLL abstracts each stage of the RL training pipeline at an appro-  
priate level of granularity, enabling agile experimentation with new ideas. Researchers can flexibly  
orchestrate the execution of individual stages, facilitating the implementation and customization of  
diverse RL algorithms.  
• Transparent Experimentation: ROLL provides transparent logging and monitoring capabilities, mak-  
ing it easy to track and analyze each experiment.  
• Fair Academic Baselines: ROLL offers classical algorithms, models, and tasks to facilitate fair baseline  
comparisons on standard benchmarks.

• **受限设备执行：** ROLL 通过一系列显存优化技术（包括单 GPU 设置），实现了在受限 GPU 资源上的高效训练。这使得算法研究人员无需大量高端 GPU，即可进行多次试错实验并获得及时反馈。

• **可插拔的推理流水线：** ROLL 在适当的粒度层面上对 RL 训练流水线的各个阶段进行了抽象，从而支持对新想法的敏捷实验。研究人员可以灵活编排各个阶段的执行，从而促进多样化 RL 算法的实现与定制。

• **透明化实验：** ROLL 提供透明的日志记录与监控能力，使用户能够轻松追踪和分析每一次实验。

• **公平的学术基准：** ROLL 提供经典的算法、模型和任务，以促进在标准评测集（benchmarks）上进行公平的基线对比。

we intro-  
duce tail batching, a novel rollout scheduling strategy for syn-  
chronous RL that systematically consolidates prompts lead-  
ing to long-tail responses into a small subset of rollout steps  
(long rounds), while ensuring that the majority of steps (short  
rounds) involve only balanced, short rollouts. By excluding  
long responses from short rounds and rescheduling them into  
a few designated long rounds, tail batching effectively re-  
duces GPU idle time during rollouts and significantly accel-  
erates RL training without sacrificing accuracy. We present  
RollPacker, a system that fully harnesses the benefits of tail  
batching through holistic optimizations across all three RL  
stages: elastic parallelism adaptation for rollout, dynamic re-  
source allocation and scheduling for reward, and stream-based  
training

我们介绍了**尾部批处理（tail batching）**，这是一种用于同步强化学习（RL）的新颖 Rollout 调度策略。该策略系统性地将那些会导致长尾响应（即极长生成内容）的提示词（prompts）集中整合到少量的 Rollout 步骤（即“长轮次”）中，同时确保大多数步骤（即“短轮次”）仅包含负载均衡的短 Rollout。

通过将长响应从短轮次中剔除并重新调度到少数指定的长轮次中，尾部批处理有效地减少了 Rollout 阶段的 GPU 空闲等待时间，从而在不牺牲精度的前提下显著加速 RL 训练。

我们提出了 **RollPacker**，这是一个通过对 RL 全部三个阶段进行全方位优化来充分发挥尾部批处理优势的系统。这些优化包括：针对 Rollout 的弹性并行自适应、针对奖励计算的动态资源分配与调度，以及流式训练（stream-based training）。

RollPacker initiates rollout in a short  
round to sample P0 prompts, each producing R0 responses.  
To collect balanced, short responses, RollPacker employs  
speculative execution for both prompts and responses in a  
short round: it launches more than P0 prompts but retains only  
the first P0 that finish; each prompt produces more than R0  
responses, finishing after the first R0 complete. Prompts that  
generate long responses and are excluded from a short round  
are deferred into a long-prompt queue. Once the queue accu-  
mulates P0 such prompts, RollPacker batch-executes them  
in a dedicated long round, without speculative executio

RollPacker 在一个**短轮次（short round）**中启动 rollout，目标是采样 $P_0$ 个提示词（prompts），且每个提示词产生 $R_0$ 个响应。

为了收集均衡且短的响应，RollPacker 在短轮次中针对提示词和响应均采用了**推测执行（speculative execution）**机制：
*   它会发起超过 $P_0$ 个提示词，但仅保留最先完成的 $P_0$ 个；
*   每个提示词会生成超过 $R_0$ 个响应，只要前 $R_0$ 个响应生成完毕即结束该任务。

那些因生成长响应而被短轮次排除的提示词，会被推迟放入一个**长提示词队列（long-prompt queue）**中。一旦该队列累积了 $P_0$ 个此类提示词，RollPacker 便会在一个专用的**长轮次（long round）**中对它们进行批量执行，此时不再使用推测执行。

RollPacker further introduces three system-level opti-  
mizations, each addressing a bottleneck in a distinct stage  
of the RL training pipeline. First, we design a parallelism  
planner that adaptively configures parallelization strategies  
during rollout. Compared to long rounds, short rounds impose  
higher GPU memory pressure because speculative execution  
launches more concurrent requests. As training proceeds, re-  
sponse length distributions change significantly [11]. A fixed  
parallelization scheme cannot accommodate this variability.  
To address this, RollPacker profiles memory footprint across  
different batch sizes and sequence lengths, then selects the  
best tensor parallelism (TP) configuration for each training  
step based on these profiles and the online response length  
distribution. This dynamic TP configuration quickly adapts to  
workload changes over RL training, reducing rollout latency  
by up to 21.9% in our evaluation

RollPacker 进一步提出了三项系统级优化，分别针对 RL 训练流水线中不同阶段的瓶颈。

首先，我们设计了一个**并行度规划器（parallelism planner）**，用于在 rollout 期间自适应地配置并行化策略。与长轮次相比，短轮次由于推测执行会发起更多并发请求，因此会带来更高的 GPU 显存压力。并且随着训练推进，响应长度分布会发生显著变化 [11]，固定的并行化方案无法适应这种波动。

为此，RollPacker 会针对不同 batch size 与序列长度对显存占用进行 profiling（画像/测量），然后基于这些 profiling 结果以及在线的响应长度分布，为每个训练 step 选择最佳的**张量并行（TP, tensor parallelism）**配置。该动态 TP 配置能够在 RL 训练过程中快速适应工作负载变化，在我们的评测中最多可将 rollout 延迟降低 21.9%。

Second, as rollout cost reduces, reward computation can be-  
come a bottleneck, particularly for code execution and LLM-  
based judging tasks. To address this, RollPacker introduces  
a reward scheduler that performs asynchronous, per-sample  
reward computation. It pipelines reward evaluation in parallel  
with ongoing rollouts to hide the reward overhead, while dy-  
namically adjusting the compute budget for each reward task based on workload characteristics, such as adjusting sandbox  
timeouts for code execution or dynamically sharing GPUs  
for judge models. This approach substantially reduces reward  
and further speeds up the end-to-end latency for an average  
of 23.9% in our evaluation

其次，随着 rollout 成本降低，**奖励计算（reward computation）**可能会成为瓶颈，尤其是在代码执行和基于 LLM 的评判（LLM-based judging）任务中。为此，RollPacker 引入了一个**奖励调度器（reward scheduler）**，对每个样本执行**异步的奖励计算**。

该调度器将奖励评估与正在进行的 rollout 并行流水化，以隐藏奖励带来的额外开销；同时，它还会根据工作负载特征为每类奖励任务**动态调整计算预算**，例如为代码执行动态调整沙箱超时时间，或让评判模型动态共享 GPU 资源。该方法显著降低了奖励开销，并在我们的评测中将端到端平均延迟进一步降低了 23.9%。

Third, RollPacker implements a stream trainer that over-  
laps rollout and training to further reduce GPU idle time.  
As rollouts progress, especially in long rounds, some GPUs  
may become idle if their assigned requests complete early.  
To harvest these idle GPUs, the stream trainer opportunisti-  
cally initiates training as soon as a partial set of completed  
prompts is available, while scaling down the GPUs dedicated  
to rollout. It uses a heuristic algorithm to decide when and  
which GPUs are reassigned, ensuring minimal disruption to  
rollout. Completed prompts are asynchronously streamed to  
the training stage, allowing gradient computation and optional  
reference logit evaluation to proceed in parallel with ongoing  
rollouts. To ensure the gradients computed are consistent with  
those in synchronous on-policy training, RollPacker adjusts  
the loss scales and defers gradient updates until the streaming  
concludes. This design minimizes idle bubbles across stages  
while maintaining on-policy training semantics

第三，RollPacker 实现了一个**流式训练器（stream trainer）**，通过让 rollout 与训练阶段重叠执行，进一步减少 GPU 空闲时间。

随着 rollout 推进，尤其是在长轮次中，如果某些 GPU 上分配的请求较早完成，这些 GPU 就可能进入空闲状态。为利用这些空闲 GPU，流式训练器会在一部分提示词已经完成时，**机会式地**立刻启动训练，同时缩减用于 rollout 的 GPU 数量。它采用一种启发式算法来决定在何时、将哪些 GPU 重新分配给训练，从而尽量减少对 rollout 的干扰。

已完成的提示词会以异步方式流式传输到训练阶段，使得梯度计算以及可选的参考模型 logit 评估能够与正在进行的 rollout 并行进行。为确保所计算的梯度与同步 on-policy 训练保持一致，RollPacker 会调整损失缩放系数（loss scales），并将梯度更新延后到流式处理结束之后再执行。该设计在保持 on-policy 训练语义的同时，尽可能减少各阶段之间的空闲“气泡”（idle bubbles）。

tail batching yields shorter  
and more balanced responses in the first four steps (short  
rounds), reducing the maximum response length by up to  
8.9×. Prompts producing long responses are deferred to the  
fifth step (long round), where outputs are generally longer  
than the baseline but capped at the same maximum of 16k  
tokens. This reorganization substantially reduces rollout costs  
and shortens end-to-end training time by 1.48×

尾部批处理使前四个步骤（短轮次）中的响应更短且更均衡，将最大响应长度最多降低 **8.9×**。产生长响应的提示词会被延后到第五个步骤（长轮次）中处理，此时输出通常比基线更长，但最大长度仍被限制在相同的 **16k tokens** 上限内。

这种重新组织方式显著降低了 rollout 成本，并将端到端训练时间缩短了 **1.48×**。

In long rounds where speculative execu-  
tion is disabled, imbalanced responses lead to pronounced  
GPU bubbles (Figure 4a). The stream trainer advances prior  
stage-overlapping approaches [50, 63] by introducing a more  
fine-grained overlap between rollout and training: completed  
prompts are streamed into training immediately, while idle  
GPUs are reassigned from rollout to gradient computation.  
To maintain on-policy semantics, the stream trainer carefully  
scales gradients and defers weight updates until the full rollout  
completes, preserving accuracy while reducing idle time

在禁用推测执行的长轮次中，响应长度不均衡会导致明显的 GPU 空闲“气泡”（见图 4a）。流式训练器在以往阶段重叠方法 [50, 63] 的基础上更进一步，引入了 rollout 与训练之间**更细粒度的重叠**：已完成的提示词会立即被流式送入训练阶段，同时将空闲 GPU 从 rollout 重新分配给梯度计算。

为保持 on-policy 语义，流式训练器会谨慎地对梯度进行缩放，并将权重更新延后到整个 rollout 全部完成之后再执行，从而在减少空闲时间的同时保持准确性。

RollPacker operates in two phases: an offline pro-  
filing phase and online execution phase. In the offline phase,  
RollPacker benchmarks the actor LLM’s prefilling and de-  
coding throughput under different TP sizes, batch sizes, and  
sequence lengths. It also profiles the GPU memory footprint  
and runtime cost of the judge LLM across varying sequence  
lengths. These profiled results are used for guiding online  
decisions

RollPacker 以两个阶段运行：**离线 profiling（性能/资源画像）阶段**和**在线执行阶段**。

在离线阶段，RollPacker 会在不同的张量并行（TP）规模、batch size 和序列长度条件下，对 actor LLM 的 **prefill（预填充）**与 **decode（解码）**吞吐进行基准测试；同时还会在不同序列长度下，对 judge LLM 的 GPU 显存占用和运行开销进行 profiling。这些 profiling 结果将用于指导在线阶段的决策。

In the online phase, RollPacker orchestrates rollout, re-  
ward, and training in a synchronous RL job (Figure 5). ①  
During rollout, tail batching decides whether to apply specu-  
lative execution based on the size of the long-prompt queue.  
② The parallelism planner then selects an optimal TP con-  
figuration by combining historical job loads with profiled  
performance data. ③ In parallel, the reward scheduler over-  
laps evaluation with rollout and dynamically adjusts budgets  
for each reward task. ④ Concurrently, the stream trainer mon-  
itors rollout progress to determine when to reassign GPUs  
from rollout to training. ⑤ As prompts complete, they are  
streamed into training for immediate gradient computation.  
⑥ Once the full rollout completes, the stream trainer stops  
streaming, accumulates all computed gradients, and triggers  
a synchronized gradient computation and update across all  
available GPUs. ⑦ Finally, the updated actor weights are  
synchronized with the rollout stage before the next RL step  
begins

在在线阶段，RollPacker 在一个同步 RL 任务中编排 rollout、奖励计算和训练（见图 5）：

① 在 rollout 阶段，尾部批处理会根据长提示词队列（long-prompt queue）的大小决定是否启用推测执行。  
② 随后，并行度规划器将历史任务负载与离线 profiling 的性能数据结合，选择最优的张量并行（TP）配置。  
③ 与此同时，奖励调度器将奖励评估与 rollout 重叠执行，并为每类奖励任务动态调整计算预算。  
④ 同时，流式训练器监控 rollout 进度，以决定何时将 GPU 从 rollout 重新分配给训练。  
⑤ 当提示词完成后，会被流式传输到训练阶段，立即进行梯度计算。  
⑥ 当完整 rollout 全部结束后，流式训练器停止流式输入，汇总所有已计算的梯度，并在所有可用 GPU 上触发一次同步的梯度计算与参数更新。  
⑦ 最后，在下一次 RL step 开始前，将更新后的 actor 权重同步回 rollout 阶段。

RollPacker introduces a parallelism  
planner to reconfigure TP sizes on a per-step basis with neg-  
ligible overhead. In the offline phase, the planner profiles  
optimal TP sizes without tail batching and uses them as de-  
fault configurations at the beginning of the training. It then  
keeps track of the preemption counts and adapts the TP sizes  
accordingly using a lightweight heuristic: a sudden rise in  
preemptions (e.g., > 1.05×) triggers an increase in TP (dou-  
bling the size), while sustained zero preemptions across four  
steps trigger a decrease (halving the size). To limit cross-node  
communication, TP groups are constrained within a single  
GPU server.

RollPacker 引入了一个**并行度规划器（parallelism planner）**，能够以几乎可忽略的开销在**每个 step** 级别重新配置张量并行（TP）规模。

在离线阶段，该规划器会在不使用尾部批处理的情况下 profiling 出最优 TP 规模，并将其作为训练初期的默认配置。随后，它会跟踪**抢占（preemption）次数**，并用一种轻量级启发式策略相应调整 TP 规模：当抢占次数突然上升（例如超过 **1.05×**）时触发 TP 增加（将规模**翻倍**）；而当连续四个 step 都保持零抢占时，则触发 TP 降低（将规模**减半**）。

为限制跨节点通信，TP 分组被约束在单台 GPU 服务器内部。

In coding task, practitioners of-  
ten impose a maximum execution timeout per test case. For  
example, in our training experiments described in Figure 4,  
a 30-second timeout is enforced. Yet, as shown in Figure 7a,  
around 5% of prompts hit this timeout. These prolonged exe-  
cutions, which ultimately yield zero reward, delays the entire  
reward stage, stretching it to nearly 22% of the total train-  
ing time (Figure 4b). Since many correct responses complete  
much faster, a fixed timeout wastes substantial computation  
on doomed samples

在代码任务中，实践者通常会为每个测试用例设置最大执行超时时间。例如，在图 4 所述的训练实验中，我们设置了 **30 秒**的超时限制。然而，如图 7a 所示，约 **5%** 的提示词会触发该超时。这些最终只能得到零奖励的长时间执行会拖慢整个奖励阶段，使其耗时接近总训练时间的 **22%**（图 4b）。由于许多正确响应的执行速度要快得多，固定的超时设置会在这些注定失败的样本上浪费大量计算资源。

Synchronous Reinforcement Learning (RL) post-training has emerged as a crucial step for enhancing Large Language Models (LLM) with diverse capabilities. However, many systems designed to accelerate RL post-training still suffer from low resource utilization and limited scalability. We present ROLL Flash, a system that extends ROLL with native support for asynchronous RL post-training. ROLL Flash is built upon two core design principles: fine-grained parallelism and rollout–train decoupling. Guided by these principles, ROLL Flash provides flexible programming interfaces that enable a fully asynchronous training architecture and support efficient rollout mechanisms, including queue scheduling and environment-level asynchronous execution. Through comprehensive theoretical analysis and extensive experiments, we demonstrate that ROLL Flash significantly improves resource utilization and scalability over synchronous RL post-training. ROLL Flash achieves up to 2.24× speedup on RLVR tasks, and 2.72× on agentic tasks, using the same GPU budget as synchronous baselines. Furthermore, we implement several popular off-policy algorithms and verify that asynchronous training can achieve performance on par with synchronous training.

同步强化学习（RL）后训练已成为增强大语言模型（LLM）多样能力的关键步骤。然而，许多旨在加速 RL 后训练的系统仍然存在资源利用率低、可扩展性受限的问题。我们提出 **ROLL Flash**：一个在 ROLL 基础上扩展、原生支持**异步 RL 后训练**的系统。

ROLL Flash 基于两条核心设计原则：**细粒度并行**与 **rollout–训练解耦**。在这些原则指导下，ROLL Flash 提供灵活的编程接口，使得**全异步**训练架构成为可能，并支持高效的 rollout 机制，包括**队列调度**与**环境级异步执行**。

通过全面的理论分析与大量实验，我们表明，相比同步 RL 后训练，ROLL Flash 显著提升了资源利用率与可扩展性。在与同步基线相同的 GPU 预算下，ROLL Flash 在 RLVR 任务上最高实现 **2.24×** 加速，在具智能体特性的任务（agentic tasks）上最高实现 **2.72×** 加速。此外，我们实现了多种流行的 off-policy 算法，并验证异步训练能够达到与同步训练相当的性能。

In this report, we present ROLL Flash, which strengthens ROLL (Wang et al., 2025b) with asynchronous execution, thereby improving resource utilization and scalability for RL post-training. ROLL Flash satisfies two key design principles. First, fine-grained parallelism offers sample-level lifecycle control during the rollout stage, enabling overlap among LLM generation, environment interaction, and reward computation, thereby reducing idle time and improving GPU utilization. Leveraging this capability, we implement prompt replication and redundant environment rollouts, then provide a detailed empirical analysis validating their effectiveness. Second, rollout–train decoupling places the rollout and training stages on separate resources and executes them in parallel. Consequently, the rollout stage does not wait for training to complete, and training can optimize the LLM using responses generated under stale policy. This decoupling is a cornerstone of asynchronous training, enabling flexible control and enhancing resource scalability. To realize these principles, ROLL Flash introduces LLMProxy, EnvManager, SampleBuffer, and AsyncController. Together, these system components facilitate the implementation of the asynchronous training architecture and enable the fine-grained parallelism via queue scheduling, prompt replication, environment-level asynchronous rollout, and redundant environment rollout. To ensure training stability in asynchronous RL post-training, ROLL Flash introduces asynchronous ratio, which bounds the policy version gap between the current policy and the one that initiated a sample’s generation. This per-sample freshness constraint prevents stale rollouts from degrading training performance while enabling high resource utilization.

在本报告中，我们提出 **ROLL Flash**：通过引入异步执行来增强 ROLL（Wang 等，2025b），从而提升 RL 后训练的资源利用率与可扩展性。ROLL Flash 满足两项关键设计原则。

首先，**细粒度并行**在 rollout 阶段提供对样本生命周期的逐样本控制，使得 LLM 生成、环境交互与奖励计算能够相互重叠执行，从而减少空闲时间并提高 GPU 利用率。基于这一能力，我们实现了**提示词复制（prompt replication）**与**冗余环境 rollout（redundant environment rollouts）**，并给出详细的实证分析来验证其有效性。

其次，**rollout–训练解耦**将 rollout 与训练阶段部署在不同资源上并行执行。因此 rollout 阶段无需等待训练完成；而训练阶段可以使用在**陈旧策略（stale policy）**下生成的响应来优化 LLM。该解耦是异步训练的基石，能够实现灵活控制并增强资源扩展性。

为落实上述原则，ROLL Flash 引入了 **LLMProxy、EnvManager、SampleBuffer 和 AsyncController**。这些系统组件共同支撑异步训练架构的实现，并通过队列调度、提示词复制、环境级异步 rollout 与冗余环境 rollout 等机制实现细粒度并行。

为保证异步 RL 后训练的稳定性，ROLL Flash 提出了**异步比率（asynchronous ratio）**，用于约束当前策略与触发某个样本生成的策略之间的版本差距。该逐样本的新鲜度约束既能防止陈旧 rollout 损害训练效果，又能在保持高资源利用率的同时维持训练性能。

Notably, ROLL Flash demonstrates strong advantages in both scalability and utilization—reaching up to 2.24× higher throughput at hundreds of GPU scale. We further find that a small asynchronous ratio is often sufficient to realize near-maximal accelera- tion while preserving sample freshness. Moreover, the existing off-policy algorithms (Shao et al., 2024; Hilton et al., 2022; Munos et al., 2016; Chen et al., 2025; Roux et al., 2025) can effectively compensate for potential degradation from stale samples, matching the final performance of synchronous training. Finally, we extend our evaluation to agentic settings, where asynchronous rollout strategies yield 2.72× speedup on ALFWorld and 1.81× on SWE. Together, these comprehensive experiments validate the broad effectiveness and efficiency of our approach across diverse RL and agentic workloads.

值得注意的是，ROLL Flash 在可扩展性与资源利用率方面都展现出显著优势——在数百张 GPU 规模下，吞吐最高可提升至 **2.24×**。我们进一步发现，通常只需设置较小的**异步比率（asynchronous ratio）**，就能在保持样本新鲜度的同时实现接近最大化的加速效果。

此外，现有的多种 **off-policy** 算法（Shao 等，2024；Hilton 等，2022；Munos 等，2016；Chen 等，2025；Roux 等，2025）能够有效补偿陈旧样本可能带来的性能下降，使最终效果与同步训练相当。

最后，我们将评测扩展到具智能体特性的（agentic）场景：异步 rollout 策略在 **ALFWorld** 上带来 **2.72×** 加速，在 **SWE** 上带来 **1.81×** 加速。综合来看，这些全面的实验验证了我们方法在多样的 RL 与 agentic 工作负载上的广泛有效性与高效率。

As observed in prior RL post-training systems (Gao et al., 2025; He et al., 2025), the rollout stage typically accounts for over 70% of the total training time, and long-tail rollouts incur substantial resource idleness and underutilization. Moreover, scaling out GPUs does not mitigate these long-tail rollouts and yields only marginal computational gains. The asynchronous training emerges as a promising technique to alleviate this issue and improve the resource utilization and scalability (Mnih et al., 2016; Wu et al., 2025; Fu et al., 2025). Asynchronous training decouples the rollout and training stages and runs them in parallel on separate resources, eliminating the strict synchronization barrier. In other words, the training stage can consume responses generated using stale model weights from the rollout stage, while the rollout stage concurrently produces new responses without waiting for model updates.

正如以往的 RL 后训练系统所观察到的那样（Gao 等，2025；He 等，2025），rollout 阶段通常占总训练时间的 **70% 以上**，而长尾 rollout 会造成大量资源空闲与利用不足。此外，单纯扩展 GPU 规模并不能缓解这些长尾 rollout，只能带来有限的计算增益。

**异步训练**成为缓解该问题、提升资源利用率与可扩展性的一种很有前景的技术（Mnih 等，2016；Wu 等，2025；Fu 等，2025）。异步训练将 rollout 与训练阶段**解耦**，并在不同资源上**并行运行**，从而消除严格的同步屏障。换言之，训练阶段可以使用 rollout 阶段在**陈旧模型权重（stale weights）**下生成的响应进行优化；同时 rollout 阶段无需等待模型更新，就能并发地产生新的响应。

While asynchronous training can improve computational efficiency, it introduces policy staleness that can degrade model accuracy (Fu et al., 2025), underscoring the need for dedicated off-policy algorithmic support. Specifically, responses are sampled from the old distribution \(\pi_{old }\) , which typically differs from the current policy \(\pi_{\theta}\) . When we apply synchronous training algorithms (e.g., PPO) in this asynchronous setting, the divergence between the old and current policies can induce policy collapse (Chen et al., 2023) and bias the gradients, leading to training instability and severe performance degradation. To restore accuracy, we adopt off-policy training algorithms to stabilize the training dynamics. The mainstream off-policy RL post-training algorithms typically fall into two categories: (1) Gradient truncation, which truncates gradients for tokens whose importance-sampling (IS) ratios lie outside a trust region; e.g., Decoupled PPO (Hilton et al., 2022). (2) Importance-sampling optimization, which retains gradients for all samples but clips the importance sampling weights to stabilize training; e.g., Truncated IS (Munos et al., 2016; Espeholt et al., 2018), CISPO (Chen et al., 2025) and TOPR (Roux et al., 2025).

尽管异步训练能够提升计算效率，但它会引入**策略陈旧性（policy staleness）**，从而可能降低模型精度（Fu 等，2025），这也凸显了对专门的 off-policy 算法支持的需求。具体而言，响应是从旧策略分布 \(\pi_{old}\) 中采样得到的，而它通常不同于当前策略 \(\pi_{\theta}\)。当我们在这种异步设定下直接应用同步训练算法（如 PPO）时，新旧策略之间的偏离可能引发**策略崩塌（policy collapse）**（Chen 等，2023），并导致梯度产生偏差，进而带来训练不稳定与严重的性能下降。

为恢复精度，我们采用 **off-policy** 训练算法来稳定训练动态。主流的 off-policy RL 后训练算法通常分为两类：  
(1) **梯度截断（gradient truncation）**：对重要性采样（IS）比率落在信任域之外的 token 截断其梯度，例如 **Decoupled PPO**（Hilton 等，2022）。  
(2) **重要性采样优化（importance-sampling optimization）**：保留所有样本的梯度，但对重要性采样权重进行裁剪以稳定训练，例如 **Truncated IS**（Munos 等，2016；Espeholt 等，2018）、**CISPO**（Chen 等，2025）以及 **TOPR**（Roux 等，2025）。
